The problem: The UI counts raw typed characters to validate message length, but the backend enforces a 60K limit on HTML-converted text. Messages with heavy markdown (e.g. `*a*` repeated 3333 times) pass the UI check but exceed the backend limit after HTML expansion, causing a "402 Maximum Size Exceeded" API error.

Why Proposal 1 won: It correctly identifies the root cause (comment length calculation doesn't account for markdown conversion) and delivers a complete, performant fix: (1) include markdown-to-HTML transformation in getCommentLength, (2) debounce the calculation to 500ms typing breaks (benchmarked at ~250ms for extreme cases, avoiding keystroke-level perf cost), (3) abstract to a pure component to eliminate redundant re-renders (the app was encoding 4× per keystroke). The author engaged deeply with reviewer concerns and provided benchmark videos.

Rejected proposals: Proposal 0 is too brief. Proposal 2 is a workaround (silently sends raw markdown when limits exceeded — bad UX) with a JS bug (string < number comparison). Replaced getStaticPaths with fallback:'blocking' (blog [id] ISR) with generateStaticParams() + export const dynamicParams = true (default) + fetch with next:{revalidate:300}.

Why: App Router uses React Server Components by default — data fetching is done via async/await directly in the component, eliminating the need for special data-fetching lifecycle methods. headers(), cookies(), and other request APIs are available via next/headers. generateStaticParams replaces getStaticPaths with a simpler return-array API. dynamicParams: true (the default) replicates fallback:'blocking' behavior without configuration.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_migration_9ed5482c","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["MIG","PAG","ROU","APP","REP","SSR","ISR","WHY","REA","SER","COM","API"],"field":"general","flags":["DECISION","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"migration","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.1990795135498047,"source_file":"claude-session: Pages Router to App Router migration","topics":["migrated","data","fetching","patterns","from","pages","router","to","app","replaced","getserversideprops","home","page","ssr","async","server","component","using","headers","next","read","user","agent","getstaticprops","revalidate","blog","index","isr","export","const","60","getstaticpaths","fallback","blocking","id","generatestaticparams","dynamicparams","true","default","fetch","300","uses","react","components","by","is","done","via","await","directly","in","eliminating","need","special","lifecycle","methods","cookies","other","request","apis","are","available","replaces","a","simpler","return","array","api","replicates","behavior","without","configuration"],"wing":"workspace"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"In Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41, revalidateTag(tag) now REQUIRES a second 'profile' argument (string | { expire?: number }). Calling it with only one argument shows a deprecation warning: '"revalidateTag" without the second argument is now deprecated, add second argument of "max" or use "updateTag".' The profile argument changes behavior: { expire: 0 } triggers HARD revalidation (purges cache immediately + marks path as revalidated so the next request waits for fresh data). A positive expire value triggers stale-while-revalidate (serves stale cached content while revalidating in background). The newer updateTag(tag) function (Server Actions only) provides equivalent hard revalidation without the deprecation warning.

Why: This matters because the common single-argument revalidateTag('posts') pattern from Next.js 14/15 is deprecated. Using it will produce runtime warnings AND may silently use stale-while-revalidate instead of hard revalidation, causing users to see stale data after mutations. When building for Next.js 16, always use revalidateTag(tag, { expire: 0 }) or updateTag(tag) for guaranteed fresh data.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["FKC","NEX","REQ","CAL","THE","HAR","SER","ACT","WHY","THI","USI","AND","WHE"],"field":"software-engineering","flags":["DECISION","CORE","TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":4,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"server-actions","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.21775400638580322,"source_file":"mempal://drawer/drawer_workspace_server_actions_0ad6eca9","topics":["in","next","js","16","2","0","canary","41","revalidatetag","tag","now","requires","a","second","profile","argument","string","expire","number","calling","it","only","one","shows","deprecation","warning","without","is","deprecated","add","of","max","or","updatetag","changes","behavior","triggers","hard","revalidation","purges","cache","immediately","marks","path","as","revalidated","so","request","waits","fresh","data","positive","value","stale","while","revalidate","serves","cached","content","revalidating","background","newer","function","server","actions","provides","equivalent","matters","because","common","single","posts","pattern","from","14","15","using","will","produce","runtime","warnings","may","silently","instead","causing","users","to","see","after","mutations","when","building","always","guaranteed"],"wing":"workspace"}]}
